# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Deployment for our Node.js microservice. Focus on getting it deployed quickly - we'll optimize resource allocation later based on actual usage metrics. The service handles API requests and connects to a MongoDB database.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: nodejsappdeploy
spec:
  replicas: {{ .Values.replicaCount }}   # Number of pods to maintain (default is nginx-node). Set this value in your values file if you want different behavior for the deployment, e.g., set it at cluster level then override per Pod's settings with `spec`
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: nodejsapp   # Selectors are used to locate pods and get information about them that kubelet knows we want (default is nginx-node). Set this value in your values file if you have a different label for the deployment, e.g., set it at cluster level then override per Pod's settings with `spec`
  template:   # Container(template) definition to create pods based on Deployment specification (default is nginx-node). Set this value in your values file if you want different behavior for the deployment, e.g., set it at cluster level then override per Pod's settings with `spec`
    metadata:   # Metadata section of a container definition to provide information about its main process (default is nginx-node). Set this value in your values file if you have different behavior for the pod, e.g., set it at cluster level then override per Pod's settings with `spec`
      labels:   # Label selectors define which Kubernetes objects to create and manage under a namespace (default is nginx-node). Set this value in your values file if you want different behavior for the deployment, e.g., set it at cluster level then override per Pod's settings with `spec`
        app: nodejsapp   # Sets label of pod to match selector defined above and manage these objects (default is nginx-node). Set this value in your values file if you have different behavior for the deployment, e.g., set it at cluster level then override per Pod's settings with `spec`
      annotations:   # Annotation provides a way to add information that won’t be stored by Kubernetes but can still provide metadata about this object (default is nginx-node). Set values in your configMap or secret. Unset if not needed, e.g., uncomment the line below and set it at cluster level then override per Pod's settings with `spec`
        # example: my-annotation: "a value"   --> To add an annotation named 'my_annotion', you can use this syntax like mentioned above in values file, e.g., uncomment the line below and set it at cluster level then override per Pod's settings with `spec`
      containers:    # Container(definition) definition to create pods based on Deployment specification (default is nginx-node). Set this value in your values file if you want different behavior for the deployment, e.g., set it at cluster level then override per Pod's settings with `spec`
        - name: nodejsapp   # Container names can be defined here to have more control over pod creation and scaling (default is nginx-node). Set this value in your values file if you want different behavior for the deployment, e.g., set it at cluster level then override per Pod's settings with `spec`
          image: nodejsappimage   # Image to use when creating container(based on Deployment specification) (default is nginx-node). Set this value in your values file if you want different behavior for the deployment, e.g., set it at cluster level then override per Pod's settings with `spec`
          ports:    # Ports to expose from inside container(based on Deployment specification) (default is nginx-node). Set this value in your values file if you want different behavior for the deployment, e.g., set it at cluster level then override per Pod's settings with `spec`
            - name: http   # Ports names can be defined here to have more control over port mapping (default is nginx-node). Set this value in your values file if you want different behavior for the deployment, e.g., set it at cluster level then override per Pod's settings with `spec`
              containerPort: 80   # Port number on which to expose http(based on Deployment specification) (default is nginx-node). Set this value in your values file if you want different behavior for the deployment, e.g., set it at cluster level then override per Pod's settings with `spec`
              protocol: TCP   # Expose port using a specific transport protocol(based on Deployment specification) (default is nginx-node). Set this value in your values file if you want different behavior for the deployment, e.g., set it at cluster level then override per Pod's settings with `spec`
          resources:    # Define resource requests and limits to be used when creating pod(based on Deployment specification) (default is nginx-node). Set this value in your values file if you want different behavior for the deployment, e.g., set it at cluster level then override per Pod's settings with `spec`
            requests:   # Resource request can be defined here to have more control over pod creation and scaling (default is nginx-node). Set this value in your values file if you want different behavior for the deployment, e.g., set it at cluster level then override per Pod's settings with `spec`
              limits:    # Resource limit can be defined here to have more control over pod creation and scaling (default is nginx-node). Set this value in your values file if you want different behavior for the deployment, e.g., set it at cluster level then override per Pod's settings with `spec`
                cpu: "100m"   # Resource limit can be defined here to have more control over pod creation and scaling (default is nginx-node). Set this value in your values file if you want different behavior for the deployment, e.g., set it at cluster level then override per Pod's settings with `spec`
                memory: "128Mi"   # Resource limit can be defined here to have more control over pod creation and scaling (default is nginx-node). Set this value in your values file if you want different behavior for the deployment, e.g., set it at cluster level then override per Pod's settings with `spec`
          imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent   # Image pull policy to use when pulling container(based on Deployment specification) (default is nginx-node). Set this value in your values file if you want different behavior for the deployment, e.g., set it at cluster level then override per Pod's settings with `spec`
